phrasal_verbs.meaning: make sb feel sad or lose hope
suggest that sth is the result of
make a place difficult or impossible to enter, leave or communicate with; disconnect
start and be likely to continue for a while (for rain, winter, an economic depression, etc)
become less noisy, powerful or active
represent (for abbreviations and symbols); put up with
destroy or remove (for buildings, statues, etc)
accept sth and try to deal with it

questions.sentence: Plans to turn much of the Thresham Valley into a large (1) **puddle / reservoir** to provide drinking water...
the environmental and economic benefits of (2) **flooding / pouring** the valley will far outweigh the disadvantages.
Several small (3) **rural / urban** communities - two villages, three hamlets and two farms - in the valley will disappear, however.
A farmer was struck by (4) **lightning / thunder** on Thursday evening...
while mending a fence in one of his (5) **plains / fields**.
It was (6) **drizzling / showering** slightly when I went out...
but I'd checked the weather (7) **prediction / forecast**...
the factory has to pump out industrial (8) **waste / litter** and that's increasing pollution...
in the (9) **suburban / surrounding** area.
We're offering a financial incentive to our customers to (10) **reuse / repeat** their bags...
An academic from Davington University has caused controversy by claiming that (11) **global / worldwide** warming is not caused by human disregard...
for the (12) **weather / environment**.
large- scale changes in the Mediterranean (13) **land / climate**, for example, have taken place ever since the world was formed.
we certainly weren't responsible for the fact the dinosaurs became (14) **extinct / endangered**...
if domestic chimneys stopped pumping out (15) **smoke / fog** into the atmosphere, the (16) **air / wind** we'd all be breathing would be much (17) **cleaner / clearer**, she said.

B.title: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)
B.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the phrasal verbs in the box.

questions.sentence: The weather should have ...... by this evening, shouldn't it?
Do you think the wind has ...... enough for us to go sailing without any danger?
Environmentalists are ...... stricter controls on the use of leaded petrol.
Why can't they ...... the fact that their products are bad for the environment?
Firefighters managed to ...... the forest fire before it destroyed any houses.
We'll have to ...... the demonstration if the weather's really bad, won't we?
The town was totally ...... for three days because of the floods.
It didn't take us long to ...... the old barn, did it?

C.title: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)
C.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: Don't throw those batteries ....... They're not biodegradable!
Rainy days always ...... me down.
Could you tell me what the letters 'CJD' stand ......?
They're planning to tear ...... the old cinema and build a new shopping centre on the land.
I think the rain's set ...... for the day, don't you?
Some scientists put the extinction of the dinosaurs down ...... changes in the world's climate.

D.title: Key word transformation (Phrases and collocations)
D.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

questions.sentence:
Josh isn't feeling very well today. (weather)
Josh is feeling a bit ...... today.

CFC's have badly affected the ozone layer. (effect)
CFC's have ...... the ozone layer.

Would you mind quickly looking at the engine? (look)
Would you mind ...... the engine?

Cleaning the beach took ages. (long)
It ...... clean the beach.

It's Carl's job to read the barometer every morning. (responsibility)
Carl ...... the barometer every morning.

There's no point trying to persuade him to recycle bottles and paper. (waste)
It ...... trying to persuade him to recycle bottles and paper.

We'll soon be able to see land, won't we? (sight)
We'll soon be ...... land, won't we?

I couldn't steer the boat because the waves were so high. (control)
I ...... the boat because the waves were so high.
The days of Athens being one of the most polluted cities in the world have **come / gone** to an end.
Their office is **at / on** the fifteenth floor.
The government's **made / done** a complete mess of its environmental policy.
It's so hot, I think I'm going to **do / have** a cold shower to cool down.
As usual, so-called nature lovers are **making / taking** a fuss about nothing.
I can't believe there's anyone in the **complete / whole** world who wants the hole in the ozone layer to get any bigger.
It looks **like / as** a large number of species will become extinct over the next decade.
You used to believe there really was a pot of gold **at / in** the end of every rainbow, didn't you?

F.title: Word patterns (gap fill)
F.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: Tourists to San Francisco are rarely disappointed (1) ...... the famous range of cultural and artistic attractions the city has to offer.
But San Francisco is more famous (2) ...... being on the San Andreas fault, a notorious source of earthquakes.
It's hard (3) ...... imagine why anyone would want to live in such a dangerous area, but millions of people choose to do so.
They are all familiar (4) ...... the faultline, and are aware (5) ...... the potential danger if there's another powerful quake.
Yet nothing, it seems, will prevent people (6) ...... building in San Francisco.
A quick glance (7) ...... a photo of the city shows a large number of modern skyscrapers, many of them completely covered (8) ...... glass.
Seismologists are constantly warning residents (9) ...... the possibility of 'the next big quake'.
They expect it (10) ...... happen sooner rather than later.
But San Franciscans are not short (11) ...... courage.
Except (12) ...... making doubly sure that their buildings are built to the highest safety standards, they carry on with their daily lives as if nothing could ever disrupt them.
